Elephant and Castle Mob.description: The Elephant and Castle Mob was an independent street gang active in London's underworld during the interwar years. They were rivals of Charles Sabini and the Birmingham Boys, and were eventually forced out by Sabini with Sicilian mafiosi. The gang achieved dominance on race courses and in London's West End from the 1910s to the 1930s, led by the McDonald brothers. They disappeared by the end of World War II.

descriptions: Kimber formed alliances with the Elephant and Castle Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Kimber formed alliances with smaller organisations such as the Hoxton Gang and the Elephant and Castle Mob.

descriptions: Elephant and Castle Mob was allied with the Birmingham Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: They were allied to the Birmingham gang, often called the Brummagems, led by Billy Kimber

descriptions: Elephant and Castle Mob was allied with the Camden Town gang.
edges.evidence_quote: They were allied to the Birmingham gang, often called the Brummagems, led by Billy Kimber; the Camden Town gang, led by George Sage and the Finsbury Boys, led by Freddie Gilbert.

descriptions: Elephant and Castle Mob was allied with the Finsbury Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: They were allied to the Birmingham gang, often called the Brummagems, led by Billy Kimber; the Camden Town gang, led by George Sage and the Finsbury Boys, led by Freddie Gilbert.

Fukuhaku-kai.description: The Fukuhaku-kai is a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka, Japan, founded in 1985 by Akira Umedu. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated membership of 60. The group has been involved in conflicts with the Yamaguchi-gumi and other local syndicates over territory in the Nakasu red-light district.

descriptions: Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with Dojin-kai over attempts to enter Nakasu territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with four different Yamaguchi-affiliates over the concessions of Nakasu , the largest red-light district in Kyushu, and also with the Dojin-kai and Kudo-kai over their attempts to enter the same territory.

descriptions: Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with Kudo-kai over attempts to enter Nakasu territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fukuhaku-kai has been in conflict with four different Yamaguchi-affiliates over the concessions of Nakasu , the largest red-light district in Kyushu, and also with the Dojin-kai and Kudo-kai over their attempts to enter the same territory.

descriptions: Fukuhaku-kai had a conflict with Yamaguchi-gumi involving firearms in 2004.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fukuhaku-kai has never been a member of this federation, however has caused at least one conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi, which involved firearms, in 2004.

descriptions: Fukuhaku-kai is an ally of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Inagawa-kai, Soai-kai, Matsuba-kai, Kyōsei-kai, Aizukotetsu-kai, Toa-kai, Fukuhaku-kai, Gōda-ikka, Shinwa-kai

Westies.description: The Westies were a New York City-based Irish-American organized crime gang operating from the early 1960s until 1988. They were involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and contract killing, and were partnered with the Italian-American Mafia. Based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan, they were responsible for 60-100 murders between 1968 and 1986. The gang was founded by James Coonan and led by Mickey Spillane before Coonan took over.

descriptions: The Westies murdered a Colombo associate.
edges.evidence_quote: Irish mobster Jimmy Coonan became Stein's bodyguard. Coonan and the Westies gang murdered Stein in 1977

descriptions: DeMeo formed an alliance with the Westies, which helped him become a made member.
edges.evidence_quote: DeMeo secured his induction into the Gambino family by forming an alliance with an Irish-American gang known as the Westies

descriptions: The Gambino family lists Westies as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Pagan's MC, Westies

descriptions: Westies leader Radonjić helped Gotti by intimidating a witness.
edges.evidence_quote: Boško 'The Yugo' Radonjić, the head of the Westies in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan.

descriptions: Gambino family brokered a peace and formed an alliance with the Westies from 1977 to 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Westies alliance (1977–1992) resulted from an ongoing war between the Genovese family and the Westies.

descriptions: The Westies became a de facto arm of the Gambino family, sharing profits and taking murder contracts.
edges.evidence_quote: DeMeo secured his induction into the Gambino family by forming an alliance with an Irish-American gang known as the Westies

Cleveland crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, formed during the 1900s. Under boss John T. Scalish (1945-1976), it exerted influence over the Teamsters and skimmed Las Vegas casino revenue. Membership peaked at 60 made men in the 1950s. After a gang war in the late 1970s and subsequent convictions, the family weakened but remains active in illegal gambling and loansharking.
